---
format: html
title: generate_qr_code()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>generate_qr_code</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Generate ORACLE QR Code</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Generates a QR code SVG for a URL in ORACLE colours and optionally
returns a knitr graphics include object.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>generate_qr_code(
  url,
  name,
  background_color,
  foreground_color,
  plot,
  base_path = here::here("Documentation", "Presentations", "IAVS_2026", "figures",
    "qrcodes")
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="url">url</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar URL encoded in the QR code.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="name">name</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar used in output file name ('qr_&lt;name&gt;.svg').</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="background_color">background_color</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Background colour passed to [qrcode::generate_svg()].</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="foreground_color">foreground_color</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Foreground colour passed to [qrcode::generate_svg()].</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="plot">plot</code></td>
<td>
<p>Logical scalar. If 'TRUE', returns [knitr::include_graphics()].</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="base_path">base_path</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar output directory for SVG files.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>Character path to generated SVG when 'plot = FALSE', otherwise the
object returned by [knitr::include_graphics()].
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
